title: Build a Responsive Landing Page
mission: Build your first React application and understand how React works.
description: Create a portfolio-worthy landing page using Vite, React 19, and TypeScript. Practice component composition, typed props, and responsive layout. This is your first GitHub project in the FrontForge track.
requirements: Scaffold a new project with Vite + React + TypeScript
Create a Navbar component with brand name and navigation links
Create a Hero component with title, subtitle, and primary CTA
Create a Features section with at least 3 feature cards from an array using map()
Create a Footer component with copyright text
Use separate files for each component under src/components/
Type all component props with TypeScript interfaces or type aliases
Make the layout responsive for mobile and desktop using CSS
acceptanceCriteria: npm run dev starts without errors
All four page sections render in correct order: Navbar, Hero, Features, Footer
Feature cards are generated from data — not hardcoded as three separate JSX blocks
Page is usable at 375px viewport width without horizontal scroll
Repository is pushed to GitHub with a meaningful README describing the project
hints: Create App.tsx as composition only — import and arrange components, avoid business logic there.
Define a Feature type and a features array, then map to a FeatureCard component.
Use semantic HTML: header, main, section, footer.
If styles feel messy, use a single index.css with simple flexbox — design polish comes later.
When stuck on imports, check named vs default export in the component file.

bonus.title: Polish pass
bonus.description: Add interview-visible quality signals without new React concepts.
requirements: Add aria-label or aria-labelledby on the Hero section
Add hover styles on feature cards and the primary CTA
Add a favicon and update index.html title to your product name
Take a screenshot for your README

debuggingScenarios.title: JSX syntax error
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Vite error overlay: Unexpected token or unclosed tag
debuggingScenarios.cause: Invalid JSX — often class instead of className, missing closing tag, or adjacent elements without a wrapper
debuggingScenarios.fix: Replace class with className, close all tags, wrap multiple siblings in a fragment <>...</>

debuggingScenarios.title: Missing return
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Component mounts but renders blank area
debuggingScenarios.cause: Function body uses { } without return statement
debuggingScenarios.fix: Add return before JSX or remove braces and use implicit return with parentheses

debuggingScenarios.title: Wrong component import
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Element type is invalid: expected a string or a class/function but got undefined
debuggingScenarios.cause: Named/default export mismatch or incorrect file path
debuggingScenarios.fix: Verify export in source file matches import syntax and path is correct relative to importer
